mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth measured by GDPC1 exhibits a positive trend that signals sustained economic expansion. Expanding real demand increases corporate earnings expectations, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-trading-day horizon without requiring a specific trigger condition.
evidence_refs: fact:Latest GDPC1 vintage shows 23536.293 billion chained 2017 dollars
failure_condition: Rank IC between GDPC1 trend and 20-day forward returns falls below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive GDPC1 growth quarters are followed by negative SPX returns in more than 45% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate crosses above 4.5% per annum, signaling restrictive monetary policy that increases the cost of capital and reduces equity valuations. This threshold triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as market participants reprice risk assets downward.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5%, S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level and 5-day returns is positive and exceeds 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: A joint deterioration in payroll employment (PAYEMS) and a rise in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals labor market weakness that reduces consumer spending power and corporate hiring confidence. This interaction-driven mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days when both conditions occur simultaneously.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS declines month-over-month AND UNRATE rises > 0.2pp in 1 month
failure_condition: When both PAYEMS decline and UNRATE rises occur jointly, S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of instances, or the interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in a logistic regression model.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declines month-over-month, indicating weakening manufacturing output and supply-side constraints. This signals reduced corporate profitability and operational efficiency, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-trading-day horizon as earnings expectations are revised downward.
failure_condition: When INDPRO declines exceed 0.5% month-over-month, S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC between INDPRO momentum and 60-day returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) exhibit a positive 3-month trend relative to their 12-month moving average, signaling robust consumer demand and economic momentum. This always-on mechanism predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as strong consumer spending supports corporate revenue growth.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 723853.0 million dollars in January 2025
failure_condition: Rank IC between RSAFS trend and 20-day forward returns falls below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or months with positive RSAFS momentum are followed by negative SPX returns in more than 48% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation measured by CPIAUCSL rises above 3.5% year-over-year, triggering expectations of sustained Federal Reserve tightening. This regime-conditional mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days in a high-inflation regime where monetary policy remains restrictive and real yields remain elevated.
failure_condition: When CPIAUCSL year-over-year inflation exceeds 3.5%, S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than 52% of instances, or the interaction between inflation and FEDFUNDS does not significantly predict returns (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) decline below 1.3 million units annualized, signaling weakness in residential construction demand and forward-looking economic activity. This threshold-triggered mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as the market reprices growth expectations downward.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1366.0 thousand units in January 2025, revised to 1353.0
failure_condition: When HOUST falls below 1.3 million units, S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 58% of triggered instances, or Rank IC between HOUST level and 5-day returns is positive and exceeds 0.04.

mechanism_narrative: The unemployment rate (UNRATE) declines below 4.0% while payroll employment (PAYEMS) grows month-over-month, signaling a tight labor market with strong job creation. This interaction-driven mechanism predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as tight labor markets support wage growth, consumer spending, and corporate profitability.
failure_condition: When both UNRATE < 4.0% and PAYEMS growth is positive, S&P 500 produces negative 60-day returns in more than 45% of instances, or the interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in a logistic regression model.
